title: teucrium scorodonia var. pseudoscorodonia
intro: Teucrium scorodonia var. pseudoscorodonia is a lesser-known variety of the Wood Sorrel plant, also known as Mountain Tea, native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems. The health benefits of Teucrium scorodonia var. pseudoscorodonia include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, while also exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial properties. Traditional herbal preparations involve making a tea from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which can be consumed as a decoction or infusion, or used topically as a poultice to aid in wound healing.
